CSCI-2961.description: Humans have become exceedingly dependent on software, which perhaps unknowingly puts an immense amount of pressure on the software development process, especially at scale. Unfortunately, malicious endeavors aside, even best efforts at producing high-quality error-free software that is bug-free and does not propagate ablism, racism, sexism, and other forms of oppression is extremely challenging. Couple this with the core challenges faced in scaling up the software development process and we have almost impossible odds of producing a "good" and acceptable software system. This course is co-listed with CSCI 4460 Large-Scale Programming and Testing; individual and team assignments with CSCI and non-CSCI majors alike are required. Readings in ethical software development will be covered and explored.
CSCI-2961.name: Ethics In Software Development [6-Week May 18-Jun 26]

CSCI-2963.description: This course introduces the fundamental principles and practices of ethical hacking and web security. Students will examine common web application vulnerabilities, including injection attacks, cross-site scripting (XSS), authentication flaws, business logic vulnerabilities, and access control weaknesses. The course emphasizes both offensive techniques---such as reconnaissance, vulnerability assessment, and controlled exploitation---and defensive strategies for securing web systems. Through hands-on exercises and guided labs, students will learn how to identify security weaknesses, understand how they are exploited, and implement practical mitigation techniques. Ethical, legal, and professional responsibilities in cybersecurity are emphasized throughout the course.
CSCI-2963.name: Ethical Hacking & Web Security [6-Week Jul 6-Aug 14]
